groups.title: Archaeology Course
groups.desc:
Questions 21-30
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.
groups.collect: Can be combined with any other subjects except $21$ Has three $22$ modules in the first semester Module 1 Title: $23$ Lecturer: Dr Morris Learning method: Lectures and practical sessions Content: Based on processes • recording • $24$ • interpretation • display Assessment: By $25$ Module 2 Title: $26$ Lecturer: Prof. Elliot Content: $27$ and development of built environments Assessment: By $28$ examination Module 3 Title: Method and Science Lecturer: Dr Thompson Content: Standard techniques in fieldwork and analysis Learning method: 50% lab work, 50% $29$ Site survey at the end of the module (the $30$ is to be announced later)

questions.title: Can be combined with any other subjects except 【   】
Has three 【   】 modules in the first semester
Site survey at the end of the module (the 【   】 is to be announced later)

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: Erm ... hello Professor, I'm Russ.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: I spoke to you on the phone last week.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Ah, yes, come in and have a seat.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: You want to talk to me about the archaeology course, right?

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: Yes, I've read the handbook, but I still have some questions and want to know more details before deciding whether to do it.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Right.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: So, what would you like to know?

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: Well, first of all, when I choose the archaeology course, can I integrate it with other subjects?

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: For example, one in anthropology?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Yes, it can be combined with any other subjects apart from classical history.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: That's because there is some overlap in the lecture times for those two courses.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: We weren't able to coordinate them.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: That's OK.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: Then about the modules, could you tell me the details?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: In the first semester, there are three modules, all of which are compulsory.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: What about optional modules?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Well, we don't offer optional modules until next semester.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: OK.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: The first module is called Object Matters, and Dr Morris teaches it.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: This module is mainly about what can be learnt from specific artifacts such as pottery and stone tools.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: That's something I've never heard about.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: I'm interested.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: How is the module presented?

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: I mean, is it all lectures?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Well, you mean the learning method.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: In this case, its lectures are combined with practical sessions, so it's a mixture.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: What about the content of the module?

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: I suppose we will be looking at different kinds of archaeological remains and how to date them, that kind of thing.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: To some extent, yes.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: But the module is based on processes.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: And it covers four aspects in detail: first of all, recording material, and then classification, then the interpretation of the data, that's how archaeologists conclude their findings, and finally, display.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Is that OK for you?

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: I think so.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: Yes, thanks!

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: Oh, one important thing, how is the module assessed?

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: Should we take an exam at the end of the semester?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: No.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: It's all based on coursework after each lecture, so try your best when the lecturer assigns you a task.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: OK.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: The second module's title is Towns and Cities.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: It is taught by Professor Elliot, the head of our department.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: And as the name suggests, the module focuses on the origins of built environments and how they developed.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: That's mainly factual, I suppose.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: It is.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: And for that reason, it is assessed by examination.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: But instead of a written exam, we set it as an oral one, as we think most students prefer this way.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: It sounds a bit scary.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: I prefer written exams.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Don't worry too much.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Most of our students find they enjoy it.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: OK.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: And then the third module is called Method and Science.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: And in this module, Dr Thompson will introduce you to the standard techniques used in archaeological fieldwork and analysis, such as excavating and dating.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: What about the learning method for this module?

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: Are there any lectures, or it's all laboratory work?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Oh, there aren't any lectures.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: It combines half lab work and half seminars.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: What about the assessment?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Oh, you are lucky; this module has no formal assessment.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: But right at the end of the module, you will have to take part in a site survey.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: That's great!

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: Where shall we go for the site survey?

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Sorry, I can't tell you the location yet.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: That would be announced later.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: The date for that is the week beginning the 10th of March.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: OK.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: That's all I want to know.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: Thank you very much, Professor.

transcript.speaker: Russ
transcript.en: Bye!

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: You are welcome, Russ.

transcript.speaker: Tutor
transcript.en: Bye!

21.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 other subjects；提示词为 subjects、except，此处应填学科名词或名词短语。
题目解析：定位词 other subjects 原词重现，男生提问中先出现学科词汇 anthropology，可先记录下来，但未听到题干信息 except，故不能判断其是否为正确答案。之后从女士的回答中听到相关信息 apart from，对应题干的 except，其后接的学科词汇 classical history 即为正确答案。

22.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 three、first semester；提示词为 modules、first semester，此处应填形容词或名词，修饰 modules。
题目解析：本题定位不难，题干的 modules、first semester、three 皆原词重现，随后出现的 compulsory 符合题干词性要求，记录下来即可。注意后面出现的 optional 一词，原文说的是下学期才会提供。

23.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 module 1；提示词为 title，此处应填名词或名词短语，表示课程名称。
题目解析：通过 first module 可以定位到该题，接着听到 is called，说明表示名称的词将要出现，可以准备抓取，Object Matters 即为正确答案。

24.analysis:
听前预测：定位词、提示词为 recording，此处应填名词或名词短语。
题目解析：本题定位需利用好题干已知信息。上一题结束后，考生可听到题干的 Dr Morris、lectures、practical sessions、processes 皆原词重现，接着听到 recording，表明该题答案即将出现，准备抓取，之后便可听到答案词 classification，记录下来即可。此题主要难点在于 classification 与 recording 前后间隔较短，考生需利用前文已知信息提前做好准备。

25.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 assessment；提示词为 by，此处应填名词或名词短语。
题目解析：定位词通过 assessed 体现出来，替换难度不大。定位到该题后，男生先提到 exam，可先记录下来，接着女士对此进行了否定，故 exam 为干扰信息，可排除。随后女士提到“It's all based on coursework ...”，可确定 coursework 为正确答案。

26.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 module 2；提示词为 title，此处应填名词或名词短语，表示课程名称。
题目解析：本题难度不大。题干的 second module、title 先后原词重现，定位到该题，接着便可听到名词短语 Towns and Cities，记录下来即可。

27.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 Prof. Elliot；提示词为 and、development、built environments，此处应填名词或名词短语，且与 development 存在并列关系。
题目解析：本题需通过前文信息提前定位，在听到 Professor Elliot 后，即可准备抓取符合题干要求的词。随后听到 origins 一词，符合要求，可以先记录下来，接着听到“... built environments and how they developed.”，与题干信息相对应，可确定 origins 为正确答案。

28.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 assessment；提示词为 by、examination，此处应填形容词或名词。
题目解析：本题定位不难，assessed 和 examination 先后出现，随后抓取到符合题干要求的答案词为 oral。注意该题需要通过否定词组 instead of 排除 written 这个干扰信息。

29.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 learning method、lab work、50%；提示词为 50%，此处应填名词或名词短语。
题目解析：原文先后出现 learning method 和 laboratory work，可以定位到该题，随后听到 half lab work and half seminars，其中 half 替换了题干 50% 这个信息，难度不大，可确定 seminars 为正确答案。

30.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 site survey、end of the module；提示词为 the、is、announced later，此处应填单数名词或名词短语。
题目解析：定位词 end of the module 和 site survey 先后原词重现，可以定位到该题。随后女士提到“I can't tell you the location yet.”，其中 location 符合题干要求，可以先记录下来。之后女士提到“That would be announced later”，与题干信息相符，可确定 location 为正确答案。
